steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: Two possessives are needed: the modeling belongs to multiple astronomers (plural noun ending in -s, so apostrophe after the s: astronomers'), and the trajectory belongs to the object (singular, apostrophe before s: object's).

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: astronomers modeling of the object's — "astronomers" has no apostrophe, so it reads as the subject of "modeling" rather than showing possession. The phrase becomes ungrammatical. Eliminated.

B: astronomers' modeling of the object's — plural possessive for astronomers, singular possessive for object. Plugging in: "according to astronomers' modeling of the object's trajectory" — both possessives correct. Correct.

C: astronomers modeling of the objects — missing both apostrophes. "objects" is just plural, not possessive. Eliminated.

D: astronomer's modeling of the object's — singular possessive on astronomer, but the passage implies multiple astronomers produced the modeling. Singular is too narrow. Eliminated.
